data.title: Why Is Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Rising Today, 3/13/26? Three Key Reasons
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) stock is up about 1% in early trading on Friday as investors react to several positive developments tied to the company's AI business. From a major new chip deal to rising excitement around its upcoming annual GTC conference, the momentum reflects strong demand for Nvidia's AI technology.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here are three key reasons the stock is gaining today.

1. ByteDance's $2.5 Billion AI Chip Project

One major driver is news that ByteDance, the parent company of TikTok, plans to build a large AI computing cluster outside China. The project will use about 36,000 Nvidia B200 (Blackwell) chips and could cost more than $2.5 billion.

Importantly, Nvidia said it has no objections to the deal, since the hardware will be installed outside mainland China and complies with U.S. export rules. This reassured investors that Nvidia can still sell advanced chips to Chinese tech companies under certain conditions.

2. Rising Excitement Ahead of Nvidia's GTC Event

Investors are also looking ahead to Nvidia's annual GTC conference, which begins March 16. The event is one of the biggest gatherings in the AI industry.

CEO Jensen Huang is expected to unveil new chip technology and software designed to support agentic AI, a type of AI that can perform complex tasks on its own. Nvidia shares often rise in the days leading up to the conference as investors expect major announcements.

3. A $2 Billion Investment in AI Cloud

Late yesterday, Nvidia announced a $2 billion investment in Nebius, an AI cloud provider. The move is part of the company's strategy to build large-scale AI infrastructure, often called "AI factories," across different regions.

For investors, this matters because it helps secure long-term demand for Nvidia's chips and software, strengthening its position at the center of the fast-growing AI market.

Is Nvidia a Buy or Sell Stock?

According to TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 38 Buys and one Hold assigned in the last three months. At $273.61, the Nvidia average share price target implies a 49.40% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: S&P 500 Rallies as Trump Says Iran 'Is About to Surrender'
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) opened Friday in positive territory after President Trump claimed in a virtual meeting with G7 leaders that Iran "is about to surrender." He added that the U.S. has eliminated so many of Iran's leaders that there is no one left to "announce surrender."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This morning, U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth said that Iran's missile volume has plummeted by 90% and that the U.S. would continue to intensify its strikes.

Iran Holds Firm as Strait of Hormuz Shutdown Squeezes Oil Supply

Despite Trump's claims, Iran has shown little indication of backing down. Earlier this week, Iran's newly appointed supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, vowed to keep fighting while keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed.

The passageway, which handles 20% of global oil flows, has emerged as a major point of leverage for Iran. Traffic at the strait has effectively been wiped out as ships avoid the area for fear of being targeted by Iran. That has led to an oil supply crunch, with gas prices rising sharply since the beginning of the conflict.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Adobe Lawsuit: $75M Settlement Set to End Years-Long Federal Probe
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) has agreed to pay $75 million to the United States (U.S.) government to settle a federal lawsuit alleging the software company misled consumers about the terms of its subscription services. The agreement could bring an end to a lengthy investigation into whether the company made it unnecessarily difficult for customers to cancel their memberships.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Federal regulators alleged that the company failed to disclose significant cancellation fees tied to one of its most widely used subscription options. The lawsuit claimed that customers who tried to end their subscriptions early were sometimes surprised by termination charges that were not prominently explained or displayed during the sign-up process.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Avalanche Sees Institutional DeFi Surge. Can AVAX Become Wall Street's Go-To Chain?
data.description:
Avalanche (AVAX-USD) is quickly emerging as a serious contender for institutional decentralized finance (DeFi). Wall Street moves slowly, but when it moves, it tends to move with conviction—and some of the biggest names in traditional finance have already begun putting real money through the network. These are not pilot programs or headline-grabbing announcements, but live transactions flowing through Avalanche's infrastructure. If this momentum holds, AVAX has a credible path to becoming one of the go-to chains for institutional capital.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Deal That Changed Everything

In January 2026, Galaxy Digital (GLXY) made history. It closed its debut CLO, the first tokenized collateralized loan obligation ever issued natively on a public blockchain. The deal was worth $75 million. Grove, an institutional credit protocol within the Sky ecosystem and formerly part of MakerDAO, anchored it with a $50 million commitment. Anchorage Digital Bank handled custody. INX managed tokenization and secondary market access. The whole structure was built to scale to $200 million as new loans come in. That does not happen on a network that institutions do not trust.

Built for Institutions, Not Retail Crowds

Avalanche's architecture is a big part of the appeal. Its subnet model, now called customizable Layer-1s after the Avalanche9000 upgrade, lets institutions build their own blockchains. They set their own compliance rules, validator requirements, and fee structures. They stay connected to the broader Avalanche ecosystem but keep sensitive data off public mempools. For regulated entities, that kind of control is not optional. It is the baseline.

The cost of using the network also dropped sharply in 2025. The Octane upgrade activated on mainnet on April 8, 2025. It introduced a dynamic fee mechanism that cut average transaction fees by 30% and slashed large transfer fees by 99.5%, bringing the cost of moving 1,000 AVAX from 0.04 AVAX to 0.00021 AVAX.

Then came the Granite upgrade on November 19, 2025. It brought dynamic block times, cheaper cross-chain messaging, and biometric authentication via Face Identification and Touch Identification. For institutions running tight settlement operations, that matters far more than any marketing pitch.
data.author: Hillary Remy

data.title: Cathie Wood Invests Over $10M in CRISPR and PAYP Stocks, Trims Stakes in TER and IONS
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made several notable portfolio moves on Thursday, March 12, according to the firm's daily trade disclosures. The funds increased their stakes in biotech and digital payment companies while cutting positions in select healthcare and semiconductor equipment stocks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What Did Wood Buy on March 12?

The largest buy of the day was CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP). ARK purchased 112,815 shares through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and the ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG) for about $5.63 million, reinforcing Wood's long-term focus on gene-editing technology.

Another major purchase was PayPay Corp. (PAYP). ARK bought 275,000 shares through the ARK Fintech Innovation ETF (ARKF), a trade valued at roughly $4.4 million, highlighting the fund's continued interest in digital payment platforms.

In addition, ARK added 52,075 shares of GeneDx (WGS) through ARKK and ARKG, worth about $4.43 million. The fund also picked up 18,820 shares of Tempus AI (TEM) through ARKG for around $991,000.

Here's What Wood Sold on Thursday

On the sell side, ARK reduced its stake in Teradyne (TER), selling 10,919 shares through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) for about $3.3 million. The move continues a recent trend of trimming the chip-testing equipment maker.

Similarly, ARK scaled back its position in Ionis Pharmaceuticals (IONS). The fund sold 43,533 shares through the ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG), a transaction valued at about $3.26 million.

ARK also trimmed 10x Genomics (TXG), selling 18,392 shares through ARKK and ARKG, worth roughly $376,000. In addition, the firm reduced its stake in Circle Internet Group (CRCL) by selling 11,194 shares via ARKK for about $1.26 million.

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.

Currently, analysts rate GeneDx and Ionis Pharmaceuticals as "Strong Buy." Among the names listed, GeneDx offers the highest upside potential of about 100%. Meanwhile, CRISPR Therapeutics, Tempus AI, Teradyne, and 10x Genomics carry Moderate Buy ratings, while PayPal is rated Hold by analysts.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Dow Jones Rebounds as PCE Inflation Surprises to the Downside
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Friday higher after a better-than-expected inflation reading from the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In January, the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Index rose by 0.3%, falling from 0.4% in December and matching the consensus estimate. On an annual basis, PCE increased by 2.8% compared to 2.9% in the prior month and below the estimate of 2.9%.

Rising Energy Prices Threaten to Push Inflation Higher

Core PCE, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, notched a monthly increase of 0.4% and a year-over-year jump of 3.1%, the highest since March 2024. Analysts were expecting 0.4% and 3.1%, respectively. PCE tracks changes in the prices consumers pay for goods and services over time, while core PCE is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation.

Several analysts have warned that prices could rise significantly during the coming months amid higher oil and gas prices caused by the U.S.-Iran war and the blockage of the Strait of Hormuz. "Gasoline prices in the U.S. are poised to rise to around $3.75 per gallon nationally in the ‌coming ⁠weeks, and it could take much of the year to trend back to pre-conflict prices near $3 per gallon," said Nationwide Chief Economist Kathy Bostjancic.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Top Investor Names the Best Vanguard ETFs to Buy for 2026 (and VOO Isn't Included)
data.description:
Desperate times call for desperate measures, and many investors are actively seeking calmer waters. The family of Vanguard ETFs, each of which combines numerous holdings into a single investment, can offer a healthy measure of protection against catastrophic losses triggered by the failure of an individual company.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Not all Vanguard ETFs are created equal, however. The firm offers a wide range of funds – from those carrying minimal risk to others that aim to deliver market-beating alpha (though the potential for losses must also be acknowledged).

Risk-averse investors might want to laser in on those options clocking in on the lower side of the variance ledger.

But which ones make the most sense? Top investor Todd Shriber has you covered.

"Pinpointing exactly when a market crash could materialize is difficult, but finding portfolio protection is easy thanks to these Vanguard ETFs," posits the 5-star investor, who is among the top 3% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

The first option that Shriber spotlights is the Vanguard Tax-Exempt Bond ETF (VTEB), which is comprised solely of investment-grade municipal bonds. While noting that bonds are a smart way to protect oneself against "market calamity," he argues that it still pays to be "selective."

On that note, Shriber likes the municipal option, which he calls an "often boring corner of the bond market." As an intermediate-term fund, VTEB checks that box. Its average duration of 7.2 years means that it is often less volatile than both short- and long-term bond vehicles.

Municipal bonds also tend to be exempt from federal taxes (and sometimes even state and local ones). Shriber also points out that VTEB enjoys other perks, as it is both "broad-based and cost-effective," with close to 10,000 municipal bonds (its AUM is more than $41 billion) and an expense ratio of 0.03%.

"Boring is beautiful," emphasizes Shriber when describing VTEB.

The next option that the investor highlights is the Vanguard U.S. Minimum Volatility ETF (VFMV). As the name implies, VFMV is made up of "minimum volatility" equities.

VFMV focuses on "defensive sectors," including consumer staples, real estate, and utilities. Shriber cautions that VFMV doesn't provide foolproof protection against losses, though he also notes that this ETF has the potential to outperform peers when the market tanks.

VFMV has almost $400 million in AUM, and its beta of 0.55 reflects the potential for some alpha as well. Though "low-vol" ETFs tend to be passively managed, that's not the case with VFMV (which has an expense ratio of 0.13%).

"That's potentially positive for investors because if volatility accelerates quickly, the fund's managers can be more responsive than index-based rivals," Shriber adds.

In short, VTEB and VFMV are great options for those investors looking to minimize their risks and "protect their portfolio," sums up Shriber. (To watch Shriber's track record, click here)
data.author: Gabe Ross
